---
title: "Bayes Review"
documenttype: Handout
author:
  - name: Saaleh Poovathumkadavil
toc: true
abstract: "A compact review note for conditional probability, Bayes' rule, and how evidence updates a prior belief."

version: [v.1.0]
date: last-modified
execute:
  echo: fenced
  freeze: auto
bibliography: references.bib
bibliographystyle: "springer-humanities-author-date"
format:
  tufte-inspired-html: default
---


# Bayes' Rule

Bayes' rule rewrites a conditional probability in terms of the reverse
condition:

$$
P(A \mid B)=\frac{P(B \mid A)P(A)}{P(B)}.
$$

Use [[Random Variables|statistics/probability/random-variables]] when the
events come from named measurements, and compare with
[[Linear Models|statistics/regression/linear-models]] when evidence enters a
prediction problem.

# Diagnostic Tests

For diagnostic testing, the base rate matters. A high sensitivity test can
still produce many false positives if the event is rare.
